name: subregion_view_length_matches
code:
proof fn phi_1_subregion_view_length_matches(
    region: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
)
    requires
        start + len <= region.len(),
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(region, start, len).len() == len,
{
}
reason: The subregion view length should equal the requested length — if `subrange` produced a different length, all offset calculations within the subregion would be invalid

name: subregion_view_byte_offset
code:
proof fn phi_2_subregion_view_byte_offset(
    region: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        start + len <= region.len(),
        0 <= k < len,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(region, start, len).state[k] == region.state[start + k],
{
}
reason: Subregion byte at relative offset k should equal region byte at absolute offset start+k — if the mapping were wrong, reads/writes through the subregion would access incorrect physical addresses

name: views_differ_allows_writable_addr_change
code:
proof fn phi_3_views_differ_allows_writable_addr_change(
    v1: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    v2: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
    addr: int,
)
    requires
        v1.len() == v2.len(),
        start + len <= v1.len(),
        start <= addr < start + len,
        forall |a: int| a != addr && (
            ||| 0 <= a < start
            ||| start + len <= a < v1.len()
            ||| start <= a < start + len
        ) ==> v1.state[a] == v2.state[a],
    ensures
        views_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(v1, v2, start, len, |a: int| a == addr),
{
}
reason: When only one address is writable and only that address differs, the predicate should hold — tests that the writable address function correctly permits targeted mutations

name: zero_length_subregion_view_empty
reason: A zero-length subregion should produce an empty view — if it had non-zero length, phantom bytes would appear in the subregion

name: adjacent_subregions_cover_range
code:
proof fn phi_5_adjacent_subregions_cover_range(
    region: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: nat,
    len1: nat,
    len2: nat,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        start + len1 + len2 <= region.len(),
        0 <= k < len2,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(region, start + len1, len2).state[k]
            == get_subregion_view(region, start, len1 + len2).state[len1 + k],
{
}
reason: A subregion starting at `start+len1` should align with the second half of a combined subregion — if subrange arithmetic were inconsistent, splitting a region into adjacent subregions would produce misaligned views
